content.text: What Happens When You Move a Resource

Azure supports moving most resource types between subscriptions within the same Azure Active Directory tenant. The move operation does not redeploy or copy the resource — it updates the resource's subscription ID in ARM's metadata layer, transferring ownership from the source subscription to the destination subscription. The resource's data (VM disks, storage blobs, database content) stays in place.

During the move, both the source and destination resource groups are locked against write and delete operations. You cannot modify either group's resources until the move completes. The duration ranges from a few seconds for simple resources (like a public IP address) to several hours for complex resources (like an App Service Environment with many apps).

attrs.alt: Three-phase workflow for moving Azure resources between subscriptions

content.text: Resource IDs change after a move. Any hardcoded references to the old /subscriptions/<old-id>/... path in application configs, scripts, or CI pipelines will break and must be updated.

Not every resource type supports cross-subscription moves. Before initiating a move, validate that the operation is permitted and that the destination subscription has sufficient quota and registered resource providers.

# Validate a move before executing it (dry run)
az resource invoke-action \
  --action validateMoveResources \
  --ids /subscriptions/<source-sub-id>/resourceGroups/<source-rg> \
  --request-body '{
    "resources": [
      "/subscriptions/<source-sub-id>/resourceGroups/<source-rg>/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/myvm",
      "/subscriptions/<source-sub-id>/resourceGroups/<source-rg>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myvnet"
    ],
    "targetResourceGroup": "/subscriptions/<dest-sub-id>/resourceGroups/<dest-rg>"
  }'

# A 204 No Content response means the move is valid.
# Any other response code contains an error message explaining why it cannot proceed.
Common Resources That Cannot Be Moved Cross-Subscription
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with VNet in same RG
Move VNet to separate RG first
Azure App Service with custom domain SSL
Migrate to ARM first using Cloud Migration tool
Register Required Resource Providers in Destination

# List resource providers needed by the resources you intend to move
SOURCE_SUB="<source-sub-id>"
DEST_SUB="<dest-sub-id>"

# Get providers registered in source
az provider list --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query "[?registrationState=='Registered'].namespace" -o tsv > /tmp/source-providers.txt

# Compare with destination
az provider list --subscription "$DEST_SUB" --query "[?registrationState=='Registered'].namespace" -o tsv > /tmp/dest-providers.txt

# Find providers in source but not in destination
comm -23 <(sort /tmp/source-providers.txt) <(sort /tmp/dest-providers.txt)

# Register missing providers in destination
for provider in Microsoft.Compute Microsoft.Network Microsoft.Storage Microsoft.KeyVault; do
  az provider register --namespace "$provider" --subscription "$DEST_SUB" --wait
  echo "Registered: $provider"
done

# Verify registration status
az provider show --namespace Microsoft.Compute --subscription "$DEST_SUB" --query registrationState -o tsv

The move itself uses az resource move, which calls the ARM moveResources API. You specify resource IDs to move and the destination resource group. All resources being moved must be in the same source resource group.
Moving a Resource Group's Contents to Another Subscription

SOURCE_SUB="<source-subscription-id>"
DEST_SUB="<destination-subscription-id>"
SOURCE_RG="rg-old"
DEST_RG="rg-new"

# Create the destination resource group if it does not exist
az group create --name "$DEST_RG" --location eastus --subscription "$DEST_SUB"

# Collect resource IDs to move (move an entire resource group's contents)
RESOURCE_IDS=$(az resource list \
  --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" \
  --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" \
  --query "[].id" \
  -o tsv | tr '\n' ' ')

echo "Resources to move:"
echo "$RESOURCE_IDS"

# Execute the move (locks both RGs until complete)
az resource move \
  --destination-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --destination-subscription-id "$DEST_SUB" \
  --ids $RESOURCE_IDS

echo "Move complete. New resource IDs are in subscription: $DEST_SUB"
Moving a Single VM with Its Disks and NIC

SOURCE_SUB="<source-sub-id>"
DEST_SUB="<dest-sub-id>"
SOURCE_RG="rg-prod-old"
DEST_RG="rg-prod-new"
VM_NAME="mywebvm"

# Get VM and its dependent resource IDs
VM_ID=$(az vm show --name "$VM_NAME" --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query id -o tsv)
NIC_IDS=$(az vm show --name "$VM_NAME" --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query "networkProfile.networkInterfaces[].id" -o tsv)
DISK_IDS=$(az vm show --name "$VM_NAME" --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query "storageProfile.dataDisks[].managedDisk.id" -o tsv)
OS_DISK_ID=$(az vm show --name "$VM_NAME" --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query "storageProfile.osDisk.managedDisk.id" -o tsv)

# Build the full resource ID list
ALL_IDS="$VM_ID $NIC_IDS $DISK_IDS $OS_DISK_ID"

# Run the move
az resource move \
  --destination-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --destination-subscription-id "$DEST_SUB" \
  --ids $ALL_IDS
Monitoring Move Progress via REST API

# The move API returns an async operation URL in the Azure-AsyncOperation header
# Poll it to track progress
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --query accessToken -o tsv)
OPERATION_URL="<azure-async-operation-url-from-response-header>"

while true; do
  STATUS=$(curl -s "$OPERATION_URL" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('status','Unknown'))")
  echo "$(date -u +%H:%M:%S)  Move status: $STATUS"
  case "$STATUS" in
    Succeeded) echo "Move completed successfully."; break ;;
    Failed)    echo "Move failed. Check the Azure portal for details."; break ;;
    Canceled)  echo "Move was canceled."; break ;;
    *)         sleep 15 ;;
  esac
done
Post-Move: What You Must Fix Manually
The move operation transfers the resource but does not migrate several associated configurations. These must be updated manually in the destination subscription.
RBAC Role Assignments Are Not Carried Over

This is the most common post-move gap. Role assignments are stored at the scope they were created (subscription, resource group, resource). Moving a resource to a new subscription does not move the assignments — they stay in the source subscription at the old scope, where they no longer apply.

# List RBAC assignments on the source resource group before the move
az role assignment list \
  --scope "/subscriptions/<source-sub>/resourceGroups/<source-rg>" \
  --query "[].{User:principalName,Role:roleDefinitionName}" \
  -o table > /tmp/pre-move-rbac.txt

cat /tmp/pre-move-rbac.txt

# After the move, recreate assignments on the destination resource group
az role assignment create \
  --role "Contributor" \
  --assignee "team@example.com" \
  --scope "/subscriptions/<dest-sub>/resourceGroups/<dest-rg>"

az role assignment create \
  --role "Reader" \
  --assignee "monitoring-sp" \
  --scope "/subscriptions/<dest-sub>/resourceGroups/<dest-rg>"
Managed Identity Permissions Must Be Re-granted

# A VM's system-assigned managed identity is preserved after move,
# but its role assignments in the source subscription no longer apply.

# Get the VM's managed identity principal ID
IDENTITY_PRINCIPAL=$(az vm show \
  --name mywebvm \
  --resource-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --subscription "$DEST_SUB" \
  --query "identity.principalId" -o tsv)

# Re-grant access to a Key Vault in the destination subscription
az keyvault set-policy \
  --name dest-keyvault \
  --resource-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --object-id "$IDENTITY_PRINCIPAL" \
  --secret-permissions get list

# Re-grant access to a storage account
az role assignment create \
  --role "Storage Blob Data Reader" \
  --assignee "$IDENTITY_PRINCIPAL" \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$DEST_SUB/resourceGroups/$DEST_RG/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/mystore"
Diagnostic Settings and Alerts Must Be Recreated

# Diagnostic settings point to Log Analytics workspaces or storage accounts by full ARM ID.
# After a move the old destination may be in a different subscription context.

# List current diagnostic settings on moved VM
az monitor diagnostic-settings list \
  --resource "/subscriptions/$DEST_SUB/resourceGroups/$DEST_RG/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/mywebvm" \
  -o table

# Delete the stale diagnostic setting (still points to old workspace)
az monitor diagnostic-settings delete \
  --name "vm-diagnostics" \
  --resource "/subscriptions/$DEST_SUB/resourceGroups/$DEST_RG/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/mywebvm"

# Recreate pointing at the workspace in the destination subscription
az monitor diagnostic-settings create \
  --name "vm-diagnostics" \
  --resource "/subscriptions/$DEST_SUB/resourceGroups/$DEST_RG/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/mywebvm" \
  --workspace "/subscriptions/$DEST_SUB/resourceGroups/rg-monitoring/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/dest-workspace" \
  --metrics '[{"category":"AllMetrics","enabled":true}]' \
  --logs '[{"category":"Administrative","enabled":true}]'
Moving Resources Between Resource Groups (Same Subscription)

Moving between resource groups within the same subscription is faster and simpler — resource IDs change at the resourceGroup segment only, and billing does not shift. The pre-flight validation and post-move RBAC steps still apply.

# Move a storage account between resource groups in the same subscription
STORAGE_ID=$(az storage account show --name mystorage --resource-group rg-old --query id -o tsv)

az resource move \
  --destination-group rg-new \
  --ids "$STORAGE_ID"

# Verify the move
az storage account show --name mystorage --resource-group rg-new -o table
az vm list-usage / az network list-usages
az keyvault set-policy / az role assignment create
Update application config with new resource IDs
Delete source resource group if now empty
The Azure PowerShell module exposes Move-AzResource, which mirrors the CLI's az resource move but integrates naturally with PowerShell pipelines, error handling, and existing automation runbooks.

# Connect and set context
Connect-AzAccount
$sourceSub = "<source-subscription-id>"
$destSub   = "<destination-subscription-id>"
$sourceRG  = "rg-prod-old"
$destRG    = "rg-prod-new"

Set-AzContext -SubscriptionId $sourceSub

# Collect all resource IDs in the source resource group
$resourceIds = (Get-AzResource -ResourceGroupName $sourceRG).ResourceId

Write-Host "Resources to move ($($resourceIds.Count) total):"
$resourceIds | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  $_" }

# Validate the move (dry run)
$validation = Invoke-AzResourceAction `
  -ResourceGroupName $sourceRG `
  -Action "validateMoveResources" `
  -Parameters @{
    resources          = $resourceIds
    targetResourceGroup = "/subscriptions/$destSub/resourceGroups/$destRG"
  } -Force

if ($validation -eq $null) {
    Write-Host "Validation passed (HTTP 204). Proceeding with move."
} else {
    Write-Error "Validation failed: $($validation | ConvertTo-Json)"
    exit 1
}

# Execute the move
Move-AzResource `
  -DestinationResourceGroupName $destRG `
  -DestinationSubscriptionId    $destSub `
  -ResourceId                   $resourceIds `
  -Force

Write-Host "Move completed. Verifying resources in destination..."
Get-AzResource -ResourceGroupName $destRG -DefaultProfile (Get-AzContext) | Format-Table Name, ResourceType

Azure App Service (Web Apps, Function Apps, App Service Plans) can be moved between subscriptions, with some restrictions. The App Service Plan and all its apps must be moved together. SSL certificates and custom domain bindings must be removed before the move and re-added afterward.

# Step 1: List App Service Plan and its apps
az appservice plan show --name "myplan" --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" -o table
az webapp list --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query "[].{Name:name,Plan:appServicePlanId}" -o table

# Step 2: Remove custom domain bindings (required before move)
az webapp config hostname delete \
  --webapp-name mywebapp \
  --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" \
  --hostname "app.example.com" \
  --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB"

# Step 3: Get the App Service Plan and all webapp resource IDs
PLAN_ID=$(az appservice plan show --name myplan --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query id -o tsv)
APP_IDS=$(az webapp list --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --subscription "$SOURCE_SUB" --query "[].id" -o tsv | tr '\n' ' ')

# Step 4: Execute the move
az resource move \
  --destination-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --destination-subscription-id "$DEST_SUB" \
  --ids "$PLAN_ID" $APP_IDS

# Step 5: Re-add custom domain in destination
az webapp config hostname add \
  --webapp-name mywebapp \
  --resource-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --hostname "app.example.com" \
  --subscription "$DEST_SUB"

# Step 6: Upload SSL certificate
az webapp config ssl upload \
  --certificate-file /path/to/cert.pfx \
  --certificate-password "<password>" \
  --name mywebapp \
  --resource-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --subscription "$DEST_SUB"

# Step 7: Bind SSL to the custom domain
az webapp config ssl bind \
  --certificate-thumbprint "<thumbprint>" \
  --ssl-type SNI \
  --name mywebapp \
  --resource-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --subscription "$DEST_SUB"

Azure Key Vault can be moved between subscriptions, but the move has a critical implication: the vault's soft-delete retention and access policies are preserved, but any Managed HSM associations are lost. More importantly, applications using the vault must update their references to the new vault URI (which stays the same — vaults are addressed by DNS name, not subscription) but the vault's ARM resource ID changes.

Key Vault DNS names (e.g. myvault.vault.azure.net) do not change after a move. Application connection strings referencing the vault URL continue to work. Only ARM resource ID references (used in role assignments and diagnostic settings) change.

# Before move: export Key Vault access policies for reference
az keyvault show --name myvault --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" \
  --query "properties.accessPolicies" -o json > /tmp/kv-access-policies.json

# Move the Key Vault
KV_ID=$(az keyvault show --name myvault --resource-group "$SOURCE_RG" --query id -o tsv)
az resource move \
  --destination-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --destination-subscription-id "$DEST_SUB" \
  --ids "$KV_ID"

# After move: verify DNS name unchanged (no app config changes needed)
az keyvault show --name myvault --resource-group "$DEST_RG" \
  --subscription "$DEST_SUB" \
  --query "properties.vaultUri" -o tsv

# Update Key Vault RBAC role assignments for managed identities
# (access policies are preserved; RBAC assignments are NOT)
az role assignment create \
  --role "Key Vault Secrets User" \
  --assignee "<managed-identity-principal-id>" \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$DEST_SUB/resourceGroups/$DEST_RG/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/myvault"

Moving resources between subscriptions in different Azure AD tenants is not supported via the ARM move API. Resources cannot cross the tenant boundary. If a business acquisition or organisational restructure requires moving workloads to a different tenant, the options are:
Redeploy the workload in the destination tenant using ARM templates, Bicep, or Terraform, then migrate data separately (e.g. AzCopy for storage, Azure Database Migration Service for databases).

Transfer the subscription itself to the destination tenant using the Subscription Transfer feature in the Azure portal — this moves the entire subscription (and all its resources) to a new tenant without requiring resource-level moves.
For Azure AD joined VMs: disconnect from the old tenant, rejoin to the new tenant, and redeploy.

# Subscription transfer (moves entire subscription to new tenant)
# This is a portal-only operation, but you can initiate it via REST:
TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --query accessToken -o tsv)

# Initiate a subscription transfer request (requires Subscription Owner role)
curl -s -X POST \
  "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/<sub-id>/providers/Microsoft.Subscription/requestTransfer?api-version=2021-10-01" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"properties": {"destinationAccountId": "<destination-account-id>"}}'

# The destination account owner must accept the transfer request within 7 days
# via the Azure portal or the acceptTransfer API before it takes effect.
Resource type does not support cross-sub move
Redeploy in destination; export data separately
A delete lock is on the source or destination RG
Remove the lock, move, re-add lock
Destination subscription lacks quota for moved resources
Request quota increase in destination first
Caller lacks Contributor on source AND destination RG
Ensure same identity has Contributor on both
Trying to move a child without its parent
Include parent resource ID in the move request

Moving resources between Azure subscriptions is a metadata operation — ARM updates the subscription ID in the resource's record without redeploying or copying data. Pre-flight validation confirms move support, quota availability, and provider registration. During the move both resource groups are locked. After the move you must manually recreate RBAC assignments, re-grant managed identity permissions, and update diagnostic settings and application configs that reference the old resource ID path. Intra-subscription moves between resource groups follow the same process but are simpler since billing does not change.
